issues.title: Initial notes
issues.author: italobusi
issues.body:
Requirements already supported by RFC8795 and RFC8795: Section 7.10, Section 7.11, Section 7.12, Section 7.14, and Section 7.15.

Requirements that need more discussion with TEAS WG to assess whether it is or not a gap in RFC8795: Section 7.5, Section 7.6, and Section 7.13.

Gaps to be addressed also for TE-centric networks or in general in scenarios where RFC8795 is implemented together with SIMAP: Section 7.4

===

Section 7.1: addressed in section 7.5 & section 7.6

Section 7.2: Supported by RFC8795 for the requirements already supported by RFC8795 (likewise RFC8345)

Section 7.3: Supported by RFC8345 and therefore by RFC8795. The description of the identify gap is not clear


Section 7.4: Gap to be addressed also for TE-centric networks

Section 7.5: Need more discussion with TEAS WG. If it is a gap, it should be also addressed for TE-centric networks.

Section 7.6: Already supported by RFC8795 and RFC8795

Section 7.7:

- relationship properties: supported by RFC8795
- TP/link role: not clear where is the gap: UNI and E-NNI are external links from a network topology perspective. Technology-specific augmentations can be different
- node role: gap not clear, access, metro and core definitions are sometimes operators specific
- layer/sub-layers: supported by RFC8795
- tunnels and paths: supported by RFC8795 and TE tunnel models

Section 7.8: requirement to be discussed

Section 7.10: already supported by RFC8345 and RFC8795

Section 7.11: already supported by RFC8345 and RFC8795

Section 7.12: already supported by RFC8345 and RFC8795

Section 7.13: Need more discussion with TEAS WG. If it is a gap, it should be also addressed for TE-centric networks.

Section 7.14: already supported by RFC8345 and RFC8795

Section 7.15: already supported by RFC8345 and RFC8795

DESIGN

Section 7.16: requirement needs clarification

Section 7.17: requirement needs clarification

Section 7.18: requirement needs clarification

comments.author: italobusi
comments.body: Some slides with the figures to be translated in ASCII art: [simap-applicability.pptx](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25503362/simap-applicability.pptx)

to be better clarified: "from the links connecting the termination points of the pseudonode and from the 'is-allowed' attibute of the connectivity matrix, as defined in {{!RFC8795}}, of the pseudonode."
Are they not the same ?

"since the provide to be supported may be different on different instances and it may depend on other attributes (e.g., the network type)."

not sure I have understood what you mean here

Split into two bullets to improve readability and rephrase as:

- the links entering to or departing from the termination points of the pseudonode 
- the 'is-allowed' attibute of the connectivity matrix, as defined in {{!RFC8795}}, of the pseudonode."

```suggestion
{{?I-D.ietf-teas-te-topology-profiles}} indicates that the YANG deviation mechanism is not applicable for TE topology profiles since the profiles to be supported may be different on different instances, depending also on other attributes (e.g., the network type).
```
